reviews.review_text: Great location as located on river and easy walk into town. Caravan spaces were a good size. Bathrooms were coded for security. A little tired but functional. Very helpful staff at reception. Did not hire ebikes as only learnt about them at time of arrival. Were fully booked and would have loved opportunity to have explored with these. Would stay again. Dog friendly.

For $165 a night. If I could have put no stars for the cabin, I would have. The cabin could have been cleaned properly. The lights could have worked, without calling the handy man, and the blind in the main living area could have had all the slates and closed correctly.
A great spot, bit run down but very central

Fantastic location to access town without driving.
Beautiful shady sites, super peaceful in terms of location but it is a popular park with good reason, so can get busy.
Older demographic,  no activities etc for children but can't go past the great location.
Lovely, easy  access to the river walk and sculpture trail.
Amenities are old but super clean.
Travelling with a disabled person, here are the pros and cons:
Disabled bathroom is huge. Lots of hand rails,  really good quality large shower chair, spotlessly clean. But....the doorway is narrow and you have to roll/walk to the very end of the ramp and take a very hard right angle to access. If you are unsteady on your mobility aid the handrail has no bottom rail so the potential to slip off the ramp under the rail exists.
To access the ramp, the pathway is too narrow for a mobility aid, you will have to "off road " on the grass.
The grass is, however, easily navigable, and the lack of kerbs makes navigating the park easy.
Would definitely stay again, site prices are very reasonable and there is a definite charm to the park and town.
Low cost accomodation for a long weekend. Cabin was nice and not too cold (for April). Location is close walk to town centre and not far to drive to wineries
Well kept caravan park close to shoping centre within walking distance .
Great park in a great location. Just no communal fire as advertised on the website.

Excellent location, in the heart of Mudgee.
Nice flat sites perfect for campervans and caravans.
Clean amenities and surrounded by nature.

Attended the 2026 BMW National Motorrad Rally. The choice of park was excellent, giving close proximity to event locations and a central start point for ride locations. The park accomodation was good and park staff were good enough to let me park my bike beneath an unused cabin shelter during a storm (I’ll bear that availability at future bookings). The cabin itself was perfect for shared  accommodation. When there was a cabin issue, it was quickly resolved. A lovely part of the region and a cabin park I recommend. ||In summary:|Cleanliness: - park was well maintained and clean|Noise: no more than expected |Staff: friendly and responsive |Value: priced on the high side, but would visit again.

this was our first time travelling in a caravan on a powered site . We stayed for 3 nights  with the first night experiencing a massive storm . the park lost power briefly and the weather was wild . No tree limbs fell and I believe everyone was safe.|the other 2 nights were great weather . I found the facilities old but clean . The showers were a bit confusing as the first one I attempted had no water supply at all - Undressed and ready to go before I found this out :( . Second shower found the water working but the water was cold - Its was not the best way to start the day after a cold and stormy night . other shower had luke warm water but the best pressure and hot water was found at the end cubicle . It would be good they could write out of order on the shower if it has no water so it stopped what I did but Im putting this down to being a rookie and on our next adventure  I will try all showers before choosing the best for me.  The location was great with the river walk right there and the CBD is about 2 blocks away - Id stay again .
Powered caravan site. Excellent location for walking around town. Easy access in side gate to the riverside walk/cycle path.
Lovely good sized van sites. Just a short stroll to town. Very friendly receptionist.
Best to make advance bookings. We just rocked in but had to move our van mid stay due to prior booking. Great staff and nice position in town to walk to most things.

Great location, but the amenities are very tired. Some lovely big shady sites.
Easy walk into town. Highly recommend.
Wonderful place to stay right in town. Staff were very accommodating after we had issues with our car. Would recommend to stay here anytime

Top location with easy walk to shops pubs and restaurants. Well maintained shady grounds with a lovely riverside walk. Friendly staff and very reasonable tarriff for a Tourist hotspot. Amenities older but very clean and great hot water. Huge well equipped camp kitchen. Very quiet.
I just love this place ,I leave with a smile everytime

Stayed with a caravan. Quiet location. Staff very friendly. Yes amenities need a face lift but were clean. There was no tap fitting to plug in water hose. So bring your own. Easy to walk to CBD. Pubs, brewery, cafes and take a walk along the Cudgegong river.

Location , probably one of the best walk to everything.
Amenities need fixing up ,a bit tired
Neighbour had 2 dogs tied up for the 4 days we were there , dog droppings all over the slab ,Not walked once , wake up barks every morning 5 am ,and no one around to reprimand.lift your game Mudgee council, it’s your responsibility.
Lawns around office very well kept , green and mowed ,watered,never saw a lawn or whipper snipper  out anywhere else . Needed a tidy up for sure .
No markings in slabs to know your spot
Not open Sunday arvo help yourself to a spot , they wouldn’t even know .
It’s a real shame as Mudgee is one of the nicest towns in NSW .
Very good value for money.  Showers are clean,  hot and powerful. Just what i needed.
Very clean & comfortable, close to main street of mudgee & plenty of sight seeing. The food was great in the Cafe & pubs we went to.

We stayed for 7 nights over Xmas 2025. We love Mudgee and this parks position to all it's amenities.  Just so very sad that the older cabins are now in desperate need of investment. Very basic inventory for self catering. Enough  but you have to be resourceful. We will return to Mudgee but might have to look for alternative accommodation which is a shame.

The park it's self is ideally situated, just a short walk to the CBD.
The powered sites, some have slabs some are grass. The power supply posts are updated but the sewer drains are old and some give off ouders, the water taps are in need of attention and maintenance. The amenities block is outdated and should be replaced it is a disgrace, dry rot in the showers, pin holes in the shower roses which hit the ceiling. But if you're after a pleasant stay in Mudgee it's as good as as free stay on the river if your self sufficient, but with the cost which was reasonable at $42 for a powered site.

Powered sites are poorly marked, and poorly set up. Very overcrowded park, with campers sandwiched in. Camp kitchen inadequate. Ladies amenities block has 3 sinks for 100 sites. You will queue. The park is expensive, charging 45.00 per night for a PSite. I would not stay there again.
Great caravan park close to town with lots of shady trees

We had Cabin 1 - needs to be retired. Very old, Vinyl floor had major splits, shower from the 70's and the oven was dirty with the door ready to hit the floor if you don't support it.
Floor moved with every foot step.
The whole cabin is well past it's use by date
Great place. Central to town. Very clean. Staff helpful and friendly.

Not allowed to have a fire at night.
Digital keypad to toilet/showers was difficult to manage for a lot of park users.
Laundry facilities were reduced as some machines were not available or broken.
The cabin used by a member for our group had mould on the ceiling.

Walking to the door of this cabin we could tell that it wasn't well maintained. The beds were uncomfortable and saggy, no lounge, just a table and chairs. Unfortunate  if you are there with a family  sitting at a dining table trying to watch the tiny television. Tiny bathroom. No wifi. Couldn't wait to leave. Won't be back.
The staff are really good. The location is awesome.
Yer not bad. But pricey for a grass site. Worst ammenties I've ever been in to this date. I liked the fire pit kitchen. Was a quiet night. The town is lovely. But park needs updating!
Basic van park close to town. Facilities are clean but need some upgrades. Grounds are tidy.

They cancelled booking leaving us stranded, but only found out on arrival - we ended up having to drive 4 hours to get home after driving 4 hours to get there. Total time in Mudgee 5minutes thanks to this place
Absolutely deplorable behaviour to take a booking and then cancel on the day we arrive. 4 hours drive with nowhere to stay. Hope you go bust with such crappy behaviour
Loved this caravan park, great location, very clean, cosy and generous! Even beautiful face washers & toiletries. Loved the town also, great place all up!!
Beautiful clean, cosy, warm and comfortable cabin. Very happy for the night.
Had a great stay handy spot great walking track out back staff in office excellent
The park is well situated close to everything and very walkable. The park itself is old, the reception had an unpleasant smell. sites are reasonably sized but there is a lot of surface water.

The amenities blocks are dreadful. So old and didn't look clean. Especially the rear amenities block. At least cut the branch away from the entrance to the ladies to allow easier access. I do not understand why the owners don't invest in an upgrade. The park was near full. Definitely not worth $42.00 for a grass/gravel site. On the upside we did meet some lovely people 👍

We had a powered concrete  slab site. Close to everything in town.  The amenities still from the 70s , plenty of showers and toilets.  The showers were hot so that was good. I think we were the only people without dogs which I think in closed and confined areas they should  not be allowed its also not hygienic and noisy..Not all dog owners are considerate and clean.. I don't think all sites are caravan friendly either.
Good park, can walk to main street

Lovely spot, very close to town.
It's not very big  but very nice.
Caravan sites are level and the park well kept.
Excellent

Close to everything
Cycleway behind
Shops walkable

Well located on the edge of the CBD.
Facilities show age, but very clean and functional.
Great position, however,  needs work. Amenities poor. Park grounds need to be mowed and tidied more often.

First cabin stank refused to stay there.  Second cabin didn't get much better but last one in Park, tired and old. Bed OK all electric fine, when having a shower felt like you could fall through the floor. We went there with a group of 29 lots of problems with most of them.we will never go back to this Park until they get NEW Cabins avoid this part go somewhere else.
Greg
Nice place

While the park is in a great location, it has definitely gone downhill since our last visit. Our site was a piece of uneven dirt. By the time we levelled our van, it was extremely hard to get in the door. For this, we were charged $42 per night. Not impressed.
Lovely park with good camp kitchen and clean amenities block.
Park and location is second to none. Awesome staff as well. We always stay here

Pros, great location
Cons, small site, dated amenities and worst of all, our caravan and brand new car got covered in some sort of tree sap

The last time my Husband & i stayed here was 22yrs ago so much has changed alot has been Updated. We stayed in Cabin P28 2 bedrooms everything was supplied although both Mattresses were quite hard. My Husband uses a CPAP machine at night, the Power points were litterally under each beds on the back wall i had to slide right under the bed to plug his machine in. Also no Power points in the living/dining room. Hopefully the Park will get WiFi connected in this day in Age majority of Stays supply this service free. A bonus we were central to shops clubs etc. All their staff were very friendly nothing was a problem overall i highly recommend staying here and will definately return in the near Future.

The 4 star rating is misleading. Yes, lovely neck of the woods, staff friendly. The amenities were awefull, so old and and delapitated, camp kitchen, vbasic and the roof leaked during the storm, so no place to take refuge.
No wifi in a RV park which claims Mudgee town name is also most disappointing. Travellers rely on that for forward planning.& totl stay posted re extreme weather conditions.
We have our own Caravan.
Dated, cramped and uncomfortable cabins are too expensive for what you get. We lost power in the morning so couldn’t cook breakfast. BBQ’s are old and dirty.
A nice experience at an old school caravan park that is not so usual these days.Very nice indeed 😍😍

We stayed for one night in cabin 11 in the park.  It was an older style cabin, but a good size & clean.  Big fridge, & electric blanket on the bed.  We got into town around midday & the girls at reception let us move in as the cabin was ready, which was very nice.  We would score this cabin 7/10.  The park was busy but quiet.  We would stay again.

Powered Van site , Park is Old school and very quiet for being in town , Yes the amenities are old but very clean , Camp kitchen had a great atmosphere and was clean , Staff helpful and friendly, Access to 6 Klms of level walking tracks at the back gate , thoroughly enjoyed our 3 nights and will come back 👍

Travelling, rang left name & number for return call. Didn't happen. Got to Park office closed. Rang number still waiting for return call 7 days later. My partner & I have a Caravan Park in Vic we RETURN all calls.
Perfect for Mum & I. 2 bedroom cabin was clean & well appointed. Lovely linen & comfy beds. Quiet location. Walk to town. Great hosts. Will stay again. Thank you!

Stunningly beautiful park with big trees, lovely large grassy sites, noisy Aussie birds, clean amenities and close to the centre of Mudgee. Feed the magpies and they will be friendly! We loved this park, highly recommended and we will certainly be back.
Nice place will be back

It's just the little things:
Front door does not glide evenly, a struggle to open.
Kitchen light duller than a candle, whereas bedroom light is so bright you need sunnies!
Bathroom taps bang so loud that it wakes the neighbours.
A missing slat in the bedroom vertical blind is enough for a guy to have a peek at us getting dressed. It's OK, I chased him off!
Ice tray was frozen in. Had to do a little defrost.
Otherwise  it was a good stay, bed very comfy, stove trays covered in alfoil, plenty of crockery & cutlery.
The van sites didn't allow me to both park & put my awning out. Staff indifferent when I complained with a real DILLIGAF attitude.  Wouldn't stay there again.

A 4.1 rating for this park is misleading. Yes a great location and yes lovely and very helpful staff, but that doesn’t make up for the absolutely less than okay amenities and the hit and miss quality of van sites, but mainly the amenities.

The shower and toilet block would have been old in the 70s let alone 2024 and no amount of fiddling with the taps made for an okay temperature. Noting there is a good camp kitchen so maybe there are plans to upgrade but the destination town of Mudgee deserves a van park that is vastly better.
Not a bad park in a handy location. Older amenities but are clean and work. We asked not to be put under a tree and we were but they happily moved us without a fuss. Would stay again
This a very nice park to stay in all amenities are of 4 star and close to shops  great place to stay
Great park, except the amenities.

We rented the last cabin which was at a reduced price and expected something(s) to go wrong. On the upside,it was comfy and clean. The problem was the shower, only 50 litre tank so lasted for one 5 min shower and then u have to wait ages for it to reheat. This was for cabin P5. I would have appreciated a heads up but you find out the cold way.
A very nice place to stay, we had a cabin which was clean, warm and had adequate facilities. Staff very friendly and helpful, have booked again next year.
Great excellent value stay in the heart of Mudgee. Highly recommended.
Excellent place to stay, cabins clean and well maintained. Central to Mudgee shops and pubs. Staff very friendly and helpful. Thank you to all staff for a wonderful holiday. Lisa
Medium size park, most sites are grass and slab, reasonable price, staff are very courteous and helpful, amenities are kept clean, 6 minute walk or 2 minute drive to CBD recommend this park.

Fantastic Park within walking distance of the main CBD of Mudgee. The Van sites are large with easy access. Ammenities are well looked after.  The camp kitchen is well maintained with an open fire pit. The office staff are extremely friendly and helpful. Nothing was to much of a problem for them.
Had my campervan, amenities clean, and dog friendly.

Stayed her in our van for two nights and no complaints. Amenities are clean, staff are very helpful. Best thing is the township is within like 5 - 10 min walking distance. Pet friendly, and the dog off leash area is within 5 mins walk from the campsite. The kitchen is nice and clean, has a fire pit, BBQ and your basic kitchen needs. Would gladly book again in the near future.
Such a good places caravan park. Right in town so walkable to pubs & shopping. We used our e-bikes to ride to nearby wineries and cheese shop. Good bike trails

Been here before so tried it once again. Not the cheapest for cabins but you pay what is demanded.  This time, as for the last visit, no complaints other than to say because this is a council run park they still can't figure out that visitors really do need WiFi. Besides that, the cabin was terrific, clean and had many comforts including an oven!|The park is adjacent to the city centre so it is an easy walk to everything you need.
Stayed in a van. Good clean park and facilities. Close walking distance to town. Just nice!

Stayed in one of the cabins was very clean and easy to keep warm had everything we needed for a long weekend. Great location close to town best value accommodation in Mudgee was a nice quite location and close to a nice walkway along the river would be happy to stay here again.
Had a great June long weekend
Great place to stay, clean as, friendly staff,just an easy walk to town centre.

Mudgee Riverside Caravan Park was excellent. Able to park up the van, sites were quite spacious and the whole park was pleasant. Had a great time in Mudgee plenty to eat and drink, along with other activities. Second time still an excellent location.

Great for a weekend getaway!
Close to the shopping district and the communal kitchen is well maintained.
Very basic cabin for the price but walking distance into town good
Staying one night on our trip
Not too bad. Nothing special, especially for the price.
Reception helpful. Good location. Good hot showers. Walk to CBD

Pulled up in caravan here for a couple of nights. Lovely, big spots with grass and trees around. Quiet. Amenities very old but clean. Really close to bike track and walk to town. Had a great couple of days here and would definitely stay again.
Very pleased with cleanliness and facilities.

The park is nice, the facilities are adequate, don’t expect Big4 or large NRMA standards.
The management is a bit lax, we had a bunch of Boomers take over the camp kitchen as their personal party room which set off the neighbouring vans dogs.
Not the restful quiet spot we envisaged.
And the office was closed at 6 when I went to ask if something could be done about the loud bunch.
Not impressed.
Great location but the cabins are tired but clean. Served our needs as we were in a conference the majority of the time.
Fantastic family cabin right on the river and a block from town. Amazing.
Needs some improvements

We stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin. The room had all we expected and was very clean. The staff were flexible and helpful to meet our requests. This caravan park is located very close to the middle of town and is a close walk to shops, food sellers, parks with playgrounds, EV chargers and clubs. The children had trouble finding the playground in the caravan park.

The caravan Park is in a great location, handy to the main street of Mudgee. The staff are very friendly and helpful.,
However the bed was diabolical, both my husband and I have come home with sore hips and the mattress has definitely seen better days, one of those where you roll into the middle. Such a shame.
Great location, but cabins poorly furnished, camp kitchen too awful to use. We often stay in cabins but this one was for the price very disappointing.

We stayed here in our self contained motorhome so didn’t use the amenities, but the location is great. The park is well treed, some with more shade than others, we had site 36, very leafy and close to the walkway on the creek, which provides a short stroll into town will access to pubs and supermarkets.
Excellent spot
Well run van park in the centre of town
Lovely park, clean amenities, great size clothes line. Awesome walking distance to town, eateries, pubs, great walking paths
All new amenities. Not busy and can go by walk to the centre.
The staff were very rude and very low quality assets in the cabin, $180 a night. No way was that worth it
Dated park and facilities, needs work and modernisation.
Great little caravan park , brilliant location in relation to town 5 minute stroll to all the essential things pub, pub and pub..
Great caravan park handy to town with good facilities at a reasonable price 👌

An oldie but definitely a goodie. Had a spacious site that was easily accessed. Just the right number of trees around to provide shade.  The amenities, while old, were clean and provided everything needed.  So good to have a site with great hot water in the showers. The operators were ever so helpful.  What is not to like about this place
Perfect location, clean amenities, great service.
Good size sites amenities old but spotless clean and the staff were great
Older style park. close to town. Good camp kitchen with central firepit. Amenities are very functional. Friendly staff.
Great place to stay if you want walking distance to main street. Caravan park has a great fire pit under cover. Stayed in my own van so didn’t use toilet and shower although they looked pretty basic.

Nice park within easy walking distance to the CBD. Sites are large and access is good. Amenities are older but clean. Loses a star due to them having beautiful King Parrots and Rosellas caged in a small aviary near the office. Sad to see their free cousins sitting on top of the cage when we passed each time.
Comfortable cabin, close to town centre

Park is getting a little old, but amenities are kept spotless. Grass van sites easy to get on to with concrete slab to set up on. Lots of shady trees that look beautiful in autumn. One street back from the main street, so everything is within walking distance. Situated by the river with a lovely paved walking path at the back of the park. You get a beautiful sunset over the hills
Stayed one night old but clean amenities only a small park but great position walking distance to shops and park
